Scenario: Invoke with a workflow path and `--port 8080` (and the `--port=N` form).
Expected Result: parseArgs returns { workflowPath, port: 8080 }; both `--port N` and `--port=N` parse to the same numeric port regardless of positional order.
Coverage: test/harness.test.ts: parses --port N and --port=N into a numeric port

Scenario: Invoke with only a workflow path and no `--port`.
Expected Result: parseArgs returns workflowPath set and port === null, so the daemon starts the orchestrator without forking the cockpit server.
Coverage: test/harness.test.ts: returns the workflow path when provided

Scenario: Invoke with no positional argument at all (empty argv).
Expected Result: parseArgs fails with CliUsageError (message 'usage: orchestra <path-to-WORKFLOW.md> [--port N]'); the daemon never reaches loadWorkflow.
Coverage: test/harness.test.ts: fails with CliUsageError when the path is missing

Scenario: Pass a malformed or empty-string positional path (e.g. `""`).
Expected Result: parseArgs treats a zero-length positional as missing and fails with CliUsageError (workflowPath.length === 0 guard).
Coverage: test/harness.test.ts: fails with CliUsageError when the path is empty

Scenario: Pass out-of-range or non-integer ports: 0, 70000, abc, -1.
Expected Result: Each fails with CliUsageError ('--port must be an integer in 1..65535'); the !Number.isInteger || <1 || >65535 guard rejects them.
Coverage: test/harness.test.ts: rejects out-of-range or non-integer ports

Scenario: Provide a valid `--port` but a workflow path that does not parse or cannot be read.
Expected Result: loadWorkflow fails (MissingWorkflowFile / WorkflowParseError) before runOrchestrator, so the loop never starts even though the port is valid.
Coverage: Waived (daemon/server lifecycle is live/manual smoke by design — no boot harness)

Scenario: Start the built daemon with a valid workflow and `--port` and confirm startup logging and cockpit loopback bind.
Expected Result: A single logfmt `event=started` line with version, workflow_path, pid, and snapshot_port is emitted; the cockpit is forked and bound on loopback for that port.
Coverage: Live/manual

Scenario: Parse a WORKFLOW.md with front matter (tracker kind/repo/api_key $VAR, relative workspace root) and a Liquid body.
Expected Result: Config decodes; $VAR resolves from env; relative workspace.root resolves absolute against the WORKFLOW dir; untouched blocks (e.g. polling.interval_ms=30000) keep defaults; body becomes prompt_template.
Coverage: test/workflow.test.ts: decodes config, applies defaults, resolves $VAR and paths

Scenario: Parse content with no front matter (no leading `---`).
Expected Result: splitFrontMatter returns frontMatter=null; an empty map decodes to a fully-defaulted ServiceConfig and workspace.root defaults to <tmpDir>/orchestra_workspaces; the whole content becomes the trimmed body.
Coverage: test/workflow.test.ts: defaults workspace.root to <temp>/orchestra_workspaces

Scenario: Decode an empty front-matter map and assert every block default (tracker endpoint/states, polling, hooks, agent, copilot).
Expected Result: Defaults match the schema: endpoint api.github.com, active_states [Todo,In Progress], terminal_states [Closed,Cancelled,Canceled,Duplicate,Done], interval 30000, hook timeout 60000, max_concurrent_agents 10, max_turns 20, retries 3, copilot command 'copilot', turn 3600000, read 5000.
Coverage: test/domain.test.ts: decodes an empty map to a fully-defaulted config

Scenario: Provide front matter that is a non-map scalar (e.g. `42`) or fails YAML parse (e.g. unclosed brace), or carries a type-invalid scalar (interval_ms: not-a-number).
Expected Result: Non-map → WorkflowFrontMatterNotAMap; broken YAML → WorkflowParseError; type-invalid value → WorkflowParseError (schema decode error wrapped). No partial config is returned.
Coverage: test/workflow.test.ts: fails WorkflowFrontMatterNotAMap on non-map front matter; fails WorkflowParseError on invalid YAML; fails WorkflowParseError on a type-invalid value

Scenario: Reference a $VAR for api_key that is unset/empty in the environment.
Expected Result: resolveOptionalValue collapses a Missing $VAR to undefined and the loader drops api_key entirely so preflight later raises MissingTrackerApiKey rather than seeing a stale `$VAR`.
Coverage: test/workflow.test.ts: treats a missing $VAR as absent api_key (SPEC §5.3.1)

Scenario: Set workspace.root to `~/wsroot` (home expansion) and separately a positive-int knob to a non-positive value (max_turns: 0).
Expected Result: `~/` expands to homeDir join; a non-positive value for a PositiveInt knob throws/decode-fails (validation rejects max_turns 0).
Coverage: test/workflow.test.ts: expands ~ in workspace.root; test/domain.test.ts: rejects a non-positive max_turns

Scenario: Provide front matter with unknown/forward-compat keys (e.g. tracker.future_field, experimental_block).
Expected Result: Unknown keys are stripped (Effect Struct default) so they cannot inject behavior; known fields still decode.
Coverage: test/domain.test.ts: strips unknown keys for forward compatibility (SPEC §5.3)

Scenario: Load a workflow path that does not exist on disk.
Expected Result: loadWorkflow fails MissingWorkflowFile whose actionable message names the path and real cause ('could not read workflow file ...'), not the generic 'An error has occurred'.
Coverage: test/workflow.test.ts: missing file → MissingWorkflowFile with an actionable message

Scenario: Provide front matter opened with `---` but never closed (unterminated fence).
Expected Result: splitFrontMatter treats everything after the opening `---` as front matter and yields an empty body; the YAML is then decoded/validated normally.
Coverage: Waived (build-script/static-serving internal branch; low value to isolate)

Scenario: Render a template using issue fields and the `join` filter on labels.
Expected Result: Strict rendering produces the interpolated string (e.g. 'Issue ABC-7: Add login [bug, ready]') with no errors.
Coverage: test/workflow.test.ts: renders issue variables

Scenario: Render a template with an `{% if attempt %}` branch for both attempt=null (first run) and attempt=3 (retry).
Expected Result: attempt=null renders the else/first branch; attempt=3 renders 'retry 3'; the attempt scalar is available and falsy-when-null.
Coverage: test/workflow.test.ts: renders attempt conditionally

Scenario: Render a template that references an unknown variable (e.g. {{ mystery }}).
Expected Result: Strict variables cause failure mapped to TemplateRenderError.
Coverage: test/workflow.test.ts: unknown variable → TemplateRenderError

Scenario: Render a template that uses an unknown filter (e.g. | no_such_filter).
Expected Result: Although liquidjs raises an undefined-filter ParseError at parse time, isUndefinedFilter reclassifies it to TemplateRenderError to match the spec grouping.
Coverage: test/workflow.test.ts: unknown filter → TemplateRenderError

Scenario: Render a template with broken Liquid syntax (e.g. an unterminated `{{ issue.title`).
Expected Result: engine.parse throws a genuine syntax/tokenization ParseError (not an undefined-filter) and is mapped to TemplateParseError.
Coverage: test/workflow.test.ts: syntax error → TemplateParseError

Scenario: Render the shipped WORKFLOW.example.md body against a minimal issue with null description, empty labels, and empty blocked_by on a retry attempt.
Expected Result: Strict render succeeds: the retry branch shows 'retry attempt 2', missing description yields '(no description provided)', and the Priority/Labels/Blocked-by sections are omitted (no unknown-var failure).
Coverage: test/example-workflow.test.ts: strict-renders the retry branch and handles null fields

Scenario: fetchCandidateIssues parses repo 'o/r', paginates open issues at per_page=100, filters PRs, and maps each via toIssue.
Expected Result: Returns normalized Issue[] for open issues only; entries carrying a pull_request member are dropped (isPullRequest filter in list()).
Coverage: Waived (Octokit transport has no mock/injection seam; pure normalization is fully unit-tested, transport covered by live smoke)

Scenario: makeOctokit installs the silent no-op logger and applies optional auth (api_key) and baseUrl (endpoint) only when present.
Expected Result: octokit.log.{debug,info,warn,error} are the silentOctokitLog no-ops returning undefined; auth/baseUrl spread in only when config provides them.
Coverage: test/tracker-github.test.ts: makeOctokit (#19) > installs the silent logger so Octokit cannot leak unstructured lines to the console

Scenario: A non-404 lookup failure (e.g. status 500) from octokit.rest.issues.get during fetchIssueStatesByIds.
Expected Result: mapError builds TrackerApiStatus{status} (message only if string); catchTag re-fails non-404, so the whole refresh fails with the typed error (no secret in payload).
Coverage: Waived (Octokit transport has no mock/injection seam; pure normalization is fully unit-tested, transport covered by live smoke)

Scenario: A 404 (vanished/deleted issue) during fetchIssueStatesByIds reconciliation refresh for one of several ids.
Expected Result: catchTag('TrackerApiStatus') with status===404 yields Option.none, so that id is silently omitted from results while other ids still resolve (reconciler treats it as no-longer-returned).
Coverage: Waived (Octokit transport has no mock/injection seam; pure normalization is fully unit-tested, transport covered by live smoke)

Scenario: A transport fault with no numeric status (e.g. network ECONNRESET / generic Error) during any request.
Expected Result: mapError returns TrackerApiRequest{message from Error.message else 'GitHub request failed', cause}; no token appears in the payload.
Coverage: Waived (Octokit transport has no mock/injection seam; pure normalization is fully unit-tested, transport covered by live smoke)

Scenario: Malformed repo slug: 'owner' (no slash), 'a/b/c', '/r', or 'o/' passed to parseRepo.
Expected Result: parseRepo fails TrackerUnknownPayload('tracker.repo must be \'owner/name\'') when parts!=2 or either side empty.
Coverage: test/tracker-github.test.ts: a malformed slug (no /) fails with TrackerUnknownPayload

Scenario: DEF-008: blank/whitespace-only tracker.repo (call sites pass config.tracker.repo ?? '') reaches parseRepo.
Expected Result: parseRepo short-circuits on repo.trim()==='' to MissingTrackerRepo (spec missing_tracker_project_slug), not TrackerUnknownPayload.
Coverage: test/tracker-github.test.ts: a blank repo fails with MissingTrackerRepo (DEF-008)

Scenario: api_key/token is only handed to Octokit auth and must never appear in logs or any TrackerError payload, even on failure.
Expected Result: silentOctokitLog suppresses Octokit's console lines; mapError payloads carry only status/message/cause (never the token); token spread only into Octokit auth.
Coverage: Live/manual: feature-inventory notes live dummy-token run produced safe tracker warnings with cockpit up; logger silencing covered by makeOctokit (#19) test, but no automated assertion that error payloads exclude the secret.

Scenario: toIssue maps number->id/identifier, body->description, html_url->url, lowercases labels, derives priority and blocked_by, parses timestamps.
Expected Result: id/identifier='42', description='depends on #7', url set, labels=['bug','p1'], priority=1, blocked_by=[{id:'7',identifier:'#7',state:null}], created_at parsed to Date.
Coverage: test/tracker-github.test.ts: toIssue > maps number→id/identifier, body→description, html_url→url and lowercases labels

Scenario: deriveState precedence: open issue with a status label matching a configured state; closed+not_planned -> cancel-flavored terminal; plain closed -> closed/done terminal; closed with terminal label picks that terminal.
Expected Result: Open 'In Progress' label -> 'In Progress'; closed not_planned -> 'Cancelled'; closed completed -> 'Closed'; closed labels:['Done'] -> 'Done'; open with no label -> active_states[0] ('Todo').
Coverage: test/tracker-github.test.ts: deriveState (§11.3) > prefers a status label.../falls back to active_states[0].../maps closed+not_planned.../maps a plain closed issue.../honors a terminal status label...

Scenario: Closed GitHub issue carrying a lingering active status label (e.g. 'In Progress') for both toIssue and toStateRef.
Expected Result: Closed precedence (#18) wins: state normalizes to terminal 'Closed', never the active label, so the worker can be stopped and workspace cleaned.
Coverage: test/tracker-github.test.ts: deriveState (§11.3) > maps a closed issue with a lingering active label to terminal (closed precedence, #18); and toStateRef > derives a terminal ref for a closed issue even with an active label (#18)

Scenario: DEF-005: a non-parseable timestamp (e.g. 'garbage-date') in created_at/updated_at would make Schema.Date reject and Issue.make die uncaught.
Expected Result: toDate maps Invalid Date to null; toIssue.created_at/updated_at are null and polling degrades gracefully instead of crashing the tick.
Coverage: test/tracker-github.test.ts: toIssue > degrades a non-parseable timestamp to null instead of dying (DEF-005)

Scenario: DEF-005: a hostile/author-controlled oversized priority label 'p99999999999999999999' (parses to ~1e20, outside safe-integer range) used as a denial-of-service on Schema.Int.
Expected Result: derivePriority returns null when !Number.isSafeInteger(n); toIssue.priority is null, so one malformed issue degrades that field rather than killing the poll loop.
Coverage: test/tracker-github.test.ts: derivePriority > returns null for a priority that overflows the safe-integer range (DEF-005); and toIssue > degrades an out-of-range priority label to null instead of dying (DEF-005)

Scenario: Null body and missing created_at/updated_at; plus repeated/duplicate blocker refs in body ('blocked by #5, blocked by #5').
Expected Result: description=null, blocked_by=[] when no refs; deriveBlockedBy de-duplicates repeated refs and ignores non-numeric '#notanumber'; created_at/updated_at null when absent.
Coverage: test/tracker-github.test.ts: toIssue > handles null body / missing timestamps; deriveBlockedBy > de-duplicates repeated refs; deriveBlockedBy > is empty when nothing is referenced

Scenario: labelNames flattens GitHub's mixed string | {name} label shapes and drops empty/null names; PR payloads are detected by isPullRequest.
Expected Result: ['bug',{name:'p1'},{name:null},{name:''},'feature'] -> ['bug','p1','feature']; isPullRequest true only when pull_request member present.
Coverage: test/tracker-github.test.ts: labelNames > flattens string and {name} label shapes and drops empties; isPullRequest > is true only when a pull_request member is present

Scenario: Issue domain schema validates the normalized shape: lowercases labels via NormalizedLabel transform and rejects a payload missing required fields.
Expected Result: decode lowercases/trims labels (['  Bug ','P1'] -> ['bug','p1']), parses ISO created_at to Date; decoding {id:'x'} (missing required fields) throws.
Coverage: test/domain.test.ts: Issue schema > decodes a wire issue and normalizes labels to lowercase; and Issue schema > rejects a missing required field

Scenario: isEligible accepts an active (Todo/In Progress), unclaimed issue that carries all required labels and has no blocking Todo blocker.
Expected Result: Returns true for an active, label-satisfying, unclaimed issue.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: selection / isEligible > accepts an active, unclaimed, label-satisfying issue

Scenario: isEligible rejects terminal-state issues, non-active (e.g. Backlog) states, and already-claimed issues.
Expected Result: Returns false when state is terminal, when state is not in activeStates, or when issue.id is in the claimed set.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: selection / isEligible > rejects terminal-state issues; > rejects non-active (e.g. Backlog) states; > rejects already-claimed issues

Scenario: An issue is missing one of the required labels (labels compared after normalizeLabel).
Expected Result: isEligible false until every normalized requiredLabel is present; case/space differences are normalized ('Ready' required, label 'ready' satisfies).
Coverage: test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: selection / isEligible > requires every configured label (normalized)

Scenario: Todo-blocker rule: Todo with an unresolved (non-terminal/null-state) blocker vs the same issue once In Progress; resolved (terminal-state) blocker clears.
Expected Result: Todo with unresolved blocker -> false; In Progress with same blocker -> true (already started, blockers no longer gate); isBlockerResolved true only when state known and terminal, false when state null; Todo with a Done blocker -> true.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: selection / isEligible > holds back a Todo with an unresolved blocker, but not once In Progress; > treats a resolved (terminal-state) blocker as cleared, and a null-state blocker as unresolved

Scenario: compareIssues total ordering: priority asc with null last, then created_at asc with null last, then identifier asc as a total tiebreak.
Expected Result: priority [2,1,null] -> ids ['2','1','3']; equal priority breaks by oldest created_at then identifier (A-1 before M-5, newest last).
Coverage: test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: selection / sort + selectCandidates > orders by priority asc (null last), then created_at asc (null last), then identifier; > breaks priority ties by created_at then identifier

Scenario: Property: selectCandidates over arbitrary issue arrays (<=30) returns only eligible issues, fully pairwise-sorted, and deterministically stable across repeated calls.
Expected Result: Every output is eligible, adjacent pairs satisfy compareIssues<=0, and re-running yields the same id order (engine-independent total order).
Coverage: test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: selection / sort + selectCandidates > property: selectCandidates output is fully sorted and contains only eligible issues

Scenario: Property no-double-dispatch: a claimed/running issue id is never selected even across arbitrary issue+claimed-id arrays.
Expected Result: No selected candidate's id is in the claimed set, and every selected candidate is independently eligible.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: selection / sort + selectCandidates > property: never double-dispatch — a claimed/running issue is never selected

Scenario: planDispatch admits sorted candidates greedily while global free slots remain, preserving priority order.
Expected Result: With global free slots N, the first N admissible (by per-state cap) candidates in sorted order are returned in order.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: concurrency > planDispatch respects the global free-slot budget

Scenario: availableSlots clamps negative availability to zero (transient running > limit), and globalAvailable<=0 returns [].
Expected Result: availableSlots(2,5)=0; availableSlots(5,2)=3; planDispatch returns [] when no global slots free.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: concurrency > availableSlots clamps at zero (planDispatch globalAvailable<=0 early-return is exercised indirectly via the property test)

Scenario: Per-state cap lower than global: a state with an explicit override admits up to its cap while other states fall back to the global cap.
Expected Result: perStateLimits {todo:1} admits one Todo then skips further Todos but still admits an In Progress; perStateLimit returns override for 'In Progress', global for 'Todo' with no override.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: concurrency > planDispatch respects per-state caps; > perStateLimit falls back to the global cap when no override

Scenario: Per-state key casing: override key 'in progress' vs issue/lookup state 'In Progress' (keys normalized via normalizeState in concurrencyContext and perStateLimit).
Expected Result: Lookup is case-insensitive: 'In Progress' resolves to the 'in progress' override; running counts are matched on normalized state.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: concurrency > perStateLimit falls back to the global cap when no override (asserts perStateLimit('In Progress')===2 against key 'in progress')

Scenario: Already-running agents count against both global and per-state budgets (runningTotal and runningByState seed the plan).
Expected Result: globalAvailable = max(globalLimit - runningTotal, 0); per-state budget starts from runningByState[state], so a state already at its cap admits nothing more.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: concurrency > planDispatch respects the global free-slot budget (runningTotal=1, runningByState todo:1 -> only id '1' admitted)

Scenario: Property: over arbitrary candidate lists and budgets, planDispatch never exceeds the global free slots nor any per-state cap, and the chosen set is an order-preserving subsequence.
Expected Result: chosen.length<=availableSlots; per-state counts<=perStateLimit; chosen ids appear in the same relative order as the input (greedy, no reordering).
Coverage: test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: concurrency > property: planDispatch never exceeds the global budget nor any per-state cap

Scenario: More candidates than free slots: the plan truncates at globalAvailable even when many per-state-eligible candidates remain.
Expected Result: chosen.length stops at globalAvailable (loop break on chosen.length>=globalAvailable); remaining candidates are deferred to a later tick.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: concurrency > planDispatch respects the global free-slot budget (3 candidates, 1 slot -> 1 chosen); reinforced by the property test

Scenario: ensureWorkspace for a new issue identifier (e.g. ABC-1) under a valid root.
Expected Result: Directory is created recursively, workspace_key equals the sanitized identifier, path is root/ABC-1, created_now is true; a second ensureWorkspace returns created_now=false (no re-create).
Coverage: test/workspace-manager.test.ts: ensureWorkspace creates the dir once (created_now toggles)

Scenario: after_create hook runs only on first creation; before_remove runs then the dir is deleted on removeWorkspace.
Expected Result: after_create executes (marker written) on first ensure but not on a pre-existing dir; removeWorkspace runs before_remove then recursively removes the dir.
Coverage: test/workspace-manager.test.ts: runs after_create only on first creation; removeWorkspace runs before_remove then deletes the dir

Scenario: Identifier '..' (survives sanitization since '.' is allowed) resolves outside workspace.root.
Expected Result: computeWorkspacePath fails with PathOutsideWorkspaceRoot; ensureWorkspace surfaces a Failure (no dir created above root). isPathUnderRoot also rejects the root itself and /ws/../etc.
Coverage: test/workspace-safety.test.ts: fails with PathOutsideWorkspaceRoot when the key escapes; test/workspace-manager.test.ts: rejects an identifier that escapes the root

Scenario: Identifier with disallowed characters (e.g. 'feature/login bug', 'a@b#c').
Expected Result: All chars outside [A-Za-z0-9._-] are replaced with '_'; sanitize is idempotent and output always matches the allowed charset (property-tested).
Coverage: test/workspace-safety.test.ts: replaces disallowed characters with underscore / property: output contains only allowed characters / property: idempotent; test/workspace-manager.test.ts: sanitizes disallowed key characters

Scenario: A configured hook exits non-zero (exit 3) or exceeds hooks.timeout_ms (sleep 5 with timeout_ms=100).
Expected Result: Non-zero exit yields WorkspaceHookFailed; the timeout fires WorkspaceHookTimeout (the interrupted child is SIGTERM'd by the Command scope).
Coverage: test/workspace-manager.test.ts: a failing hook surfaces WorkspaceHookFailed; a hook exceeding the timeout surfaces WorkspaceHookTimeout

Scenario: A chatty multi-line hook (60 echoed lines) writes far more than the log budget.
Expected Result: Child stdout/stderr is piped (not inherited); captured output is truncated via truncateOneLine to a single line <= DEFAULT_MAX_LEN ending in the ellipsis, surfaced as one debug record only.
Coverage: test/workspace-manager.test.ts: captures and truncates hook output instead of inheriting fds (#20)

Scenario: cleanupTerminalWorkspaces for a mix of existing and never-created identifiers (T-1, T-2 exist; T-3 missing); runHook for an unset hook.
Expected Result: Only existing dirs are removed and their keys returned (T-1, T-2); missing T-3 is silently skipped. An unset hook is a no-op success even for a nonexistent path.
Coverage: test/workspace-manager.test.ts: cleanupTerminalWorkspaces removes existing dirs and returns keys; runHook is a no-op when the hook is unset

Scenario: before_remove hook fails during removeWorkspace (best-effort teardown).
Expected Result: Per source, the before_remove failure is caught and logged as a warning, and fs.remove still deletes the dir so teardown never strands the workspace.
Coverage: Waived (subprocess/loop fault-injection path; covered via fake-script e2e + live smoke; deeper injection deferred)

Scenario: A clean Copilot turn: session.idle (dropped) then assistant.message then result exitCode 0.
Expected Result: Stream emits SessionStarted first, then AgentMessage and Notification, ending in TurnCompleted; OS cwd is the workspace (cwd.txt written at workspace path, Safety Invariant 1).
Coverage: test/agent-copilot.test.ts: streams SessionStarted → events → TurnCompleted on a clean turn

Scenario: The subprocess crashes (prints partial assistant.message then exit 5 with no successful result).
Expected Result: No completed terminal was seen, so finalize fails the stream with AgentProcessExit (code from process exit).
Coverage: test/agent-copilot.test.ts: fails the stream with AgentProcessExit when the process crashes

Scenario: A session.error line arrives mid-stream even though the process later exits 0.
Expected Result: The mapped failed terminal fails the stream with TurnFailed before the result; runCollect surfaces Failure carrying TurnFailed.
Coverage: test/agent-copilot.test.ts: fails the stream when a session.error terminal arrives

Scenario: The terminal result line is emitted with NO trailing newline (printf, not echo).
Expected Result: Stream.splitLines flushes the unterminated final segment, TurnCompleted is recognized, and the turn succeeds rather than being misread as a crash (#22).
Coverage: test/agent-copilot.test.ts: recognizes a final result line with no trailing newline (#22)

Scenario: Child env scrubbing: a daemon-only secret (ORCHESTRA_COCKPIT_TOKEN) and connectivity settings (HTTPS_PROXY) are present in the parent env.
Expected Result: Non-passthrough vars are blanked (cockpit token reads empty in child), GITHUB_TOKEN is set to the resolved tracker api_key, and HTTPS_PROXY passes through; token is only handed via env, never logged.
Coverage: test/agent-copilot.test.ts: scrubs daemon-only secrets but preserves connectivity env in the child process

Scenario: A worker running a hanging Copilot (exec sleep 30) is interrupted (stall kill / shutdown).
Expected Result: Interrupting the worker closes the run Scope whose finalizer SIGTERMs the spawned PID; the exact child PID (captured from SessionStarted) becomes dead, no orphan (#22).
Coverage: test/agent-copilot.test.ts: interrupting a worker SIGTERMs the subprocess (no orphan) (#22)

Scenario: The configured copilot.command does not exist / cannot be spawned.
Expected Result: Per source, Command.start maps the spawn fault to AgentNotFound carrying the command name, failing the stream.
Coverage: Waived (subprocess/loop fault-injection path; covered via fake-script e2e + live smoke; deeper injection deferred)

Scenario: copilot.turn_timeout_ms > 0 and the turn outlives the deadline; vs turn_timeout_ms <= 0 (disabled).
Expected Result: Per source, when turn_timeout_ms > 0 interruptWhen fails the stream with TurnTimeout after the deadline; when <= 0 no timeout guard is attached.
Coverage: Waived (subprocess/loop fault-injection path; covered via fake-script e2e + live smoke; deeper injection deferred)

Scenario: Map assistant.message with role+content, and a successful result (exitCode 0) with usage.
Expected Result: assistant.message yields [AgentMessage, Notification] (Notification only when text non-empty); result exitCode 0 yields TurnCompleted with completed terminal and mapped camelCase usage.
Coverage: test/agent-copilot.test.ts: maps assistant.message to AgentMessage + Notification; emits only AgentMessage when the message has no text; maps a successful result to TurnCompleted + completed terminal

Scenario: Map terminal failure events: result exitCode 2, session.error, model.call_failure, and turn_input_required.
Expected Result: Non-zero result -> failed terminal AgentProcessExit (no events); session.error -> TurnFailed; model.call_failure -> TurnEndedWithError event + ResponseError terminal; turn_input_required -> TurnInputRequired terminal.
Coverage: test/agent-copilot.test.ts: maps a non-zero result to a failed terminal (AgentProcessExit); maps session.error to TurnFailed + failed terminal; maps model.call_failure to TurnEndedWithError + ResponseError terminal; maps turn_input_required to a failed terminal (TurnInputRequired)

Scenario: Unparseable JSON, a typeless object ({data:{}}), and an empty-type line.
Expected Result: Mapper never throws: unparseable -> Malformed; object with no type ('' type) -> Malformed (visibility preserved); blank line -> no events.
Coverage: test/agent-copilot.test.ts: drops blank lines; maps unparseable lines to Malformed; maps a typeless object to Malformed

Scenario: A result whose exitCode is present but non-numeric (the string '5') (DEF-006).
Expected Result: Present-but-non-numeric/non-finite exitCode maps to 1 (failure), not a clean turn, so a failed turn is not masked; terminal is failed AgentProcessExit. Absent exitCode keeps the historical completed default.
Coverage: test/agent-copilot.test.ts: treats a present-but-non-numeric exitCode as a failure, not success (DEF-006); keeps the completed default for a result with no exitCode field

Scenario: Usage carries a non-finite numeric (totalApiDurationMs=1e400 -> Infinity, outputTokens=NaN) (DEF-002).
Expected Result: num() is finite-only: non-finite fields are dropped from usage (so total_api_duration_ms is undefined), preventing a null runtime_seconds that would discard the whole checkpoint on next boot; finite siblings survive.
Coverage: test/agent-copilot.test.ts: drops an overflowing duration parsed from a raw result line (DEF-002); drops non-finite numeric fields so they can't corrupt the durable checkpoint (DEF-002)

Scenario: mapUsage with all camelCase fields populated vs an empty/null object.
Expected Result: Full set maps to normalized snake_case fields; an object with nothing usable returns undefined (and null returns undefined).
Coverage: test/agent-copilot.test.ts: maps camelCase Copilot usage to normalized fields; returns undefined when nothing usable is present

Scenario: permission.* events and recognized-but-unsurfaced events (session.idle, assistant.turn_start, assistant.message_delta).
Expected Result: permission.* -> ApprovalAutoApproved surfacing the auto-grant; recognized noise/deltas -> dropped (empty events) without crashing.
Coverage: test/agent-copilot.test.ts: maps permission.* to ApprovalAutoApproved; drops recognized-but-unsurfaced events without crashing

Scenario: A line whose timestamp is missing or an invalid date string.
Expected Result: parseTimestamp falls back to the injected 'now' when timestamp is absent or NaN; the mapper never throws on a bad timestamp.
Coverage: test/agent-copilot.test.ts: falls back to the injected now for a missing or invalid timestamp

Scenario: A single eligible candidate dispatches, streams events, completes at max_turns=1, and is recorded.
Expected Result: Worker runs before_run/after_run hooks, usage is folded into agent_totals, WorkerCompleted fires, completed contains the id, running is cleared, and a rich RecentCompletions entry (outcome 'completed') is recorded.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-loop.test.ts: dispatch → success: single turn completes and is recorded; test/e2e-fake.test.ts: two issues are selected, run, and completed; snapshot reflects the result

Scenario: Concurrency cap of 1 with two eligible issues; one is closed out so a slot frees.
Expected Result: Only one issue dispatches per tick; after the first is reconciled away (terminal), the next tick dispatches the second issue into the freed slot.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-loop.test.ts: concurrency cap of 1 dispatches one issue, requeues the next after a slot frees

Scenario: Per-tick preflight failure or tracker fetchCandidateIssues error.
Expected Result: Reconcile still runs, then PreflightFailed/TrackerError is emitted and TickEnd reports dispatchSkipped=true with no dispatches; in-flight work is untouched.
Coverage: Waived (subprocess/loop fault-injection path; covered via fake-script e2e + live smoke; deeper injection deferred)

Scenario: Startup: loop restores state, runs startupCleanup over terminal issues, emits Started, enqueues the immediate Tick, then polls every poll_interval_ms.
Expected Result: Started observation carries the effective poll interval and concurrency; terminal workspaces are cleaned (StartupCleanup); the first Tick reconciles then dispatches.
Coverage: test/restore-reconcile.test.ts: missing checkpoint → clean empty boot, normal dispatch (regression guard)

Scenario: An operator command races a tick / worker-done (single state-owning fiber invariant).
Expected Result: Commands pumped off the CommandBus flow through the same single mailbox and are applied serially by the owner fiber; only that fiber mutates state, so commands never race ticks or each other.
Coverage: test/command-control.test.ts: PauseDispatch withholds new dispatch; an in-flight worker is untouched; CancelSession interrupts only the named worker; RetryNow fires a pending retry early

Scenario: CancelSession for a tracked issue vs an unknown id; a timed-out (interrupted) command in the bus.
Expected Result: CancelSession interrupts only that worker+timer, releases the issue (not completed), and drops its registry entry; unknown id is a typed Ack(accepted=false); an abandoned (interrupted) command is dropped (reply isDone) without mutating state, while a later normal command applies once.
Coverage: test/command-control.test.ts: CancelSession interrupts only the named worker; a timed-out (interrupted) command is dropped, not applied; a normal one still applies once

Scenario: Shutdown: the orchestrator fiber is interrupted while workers and timers are live.
Expected Result: Interrupting the loop fiber tears down the internal Scope and with it the scheduler, every worker fiber, and every retry timer (forkScoped under Effect.scoped); interrupted workers post no spurious WorkerDone.
Coverage: Live/manual

Scenario: Clean turn below max_turns schedules a short continuation retry, then re-dispatches resuming the session.
Expected Result: After turn 1 completes (max_turns=2) a continuation retry (delay 1000ms) is scheduled; firing it re-dispatches turn 2 with resumeSessionId carried from the prior SessionStarted.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-loop.test.ts: dispatch → continuation: second turn resumes the session

Scenario: A failed turn schedules an exponential failure backoff (10000ms first), which fires and re-dispatches fresh, then completes.
Expected Result: WorkerFailed -> RetryScheduled(kind=failure, delayMs=10000); after the 10s backoff the retry re-dispatches a FRESH session (resumeSessionId null) with attempt=1 and succeeds.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-loop.test.ts: failure → exponential backoff retry then success

Scenario: failureBackoffMs across attempt values: 1,2,3,99 with cap; attempt <= 0; very large attempts overflowing 2^(attempt-1).
Expected Result: Returns min(10000*2^(attempt-1), cap): 10000/20000/40000, capped at 300000 for 99; attempt<=0 treated as first attempt; large attempts overflow to Infinity and clamp to the cap; monotonic non-decreasing, never above cap (property).
Coverage: test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: uses 10s * 2^(attempt-1), capped; treats attempt <= 0 as the first attempt; property: monotonic non-decreasing and never above the cap

Scenario: Clean completion at max_turns (turnCount reaches max_turns).
Expected Result: No continuation is scheduled; the issue is markCompleted, recorded in RecentCompletions, registry entry dropped, and WorkerCompleted emitted.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-loop.test.ts: dispatch → success: single turn completes and is recorded (max_turns=1 hits the cap on turn 1)

Scenario: Operator RetryNow fires a pending backoff at the exact moment the armed timer also posts its own RetryDue (exactly-once race).
Expected Result: RetryNow interrupts the armed timer and re-dispatches; the now-stale RetryDue is dropped by handleRetryDue (rec.workerFiber !== null guard) so exactly two runs occur, no orphaned second worker.
Coverage: test/command-control.test.ts: RetryNow racing a fired backoff timer dispatches exactly one worker; RetryNow fires a pending retry early; unknown id is a typed no-op

Scenario: A retry timer fires for an issue that is no longer active (released/reconciled away during the backoff window).
Expected Result: handleRetryDue is idempotent: if the registry entry is gone (rec undefined) it returns without dispatching; a retrying issue that went terminal mid-backoff is reconciled (killed) and never re-dispatched.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-loop.test.ts: a retrying issue that goes terminal mid-backoff is reconciled, not re-dispatched (Fixes #17)

Scenario: A retrying issue holds its concurrency slot across the backoff window (cap honesty, #17).
Expected Result: occupiesSlot counts a pending timer, so a tick landing inside the backoff plans zero dispatches (cap=1) and the issue re-dispatches into its own reserved slot, never over-admitting another issue.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-loop.test.ts: concurrency cap is held across a retry backoff: a retrying issue reserves its slot (Fixes #17)

Scenario: Reconcile sees a running issue gone terminal in the tracker.
Expected Result: TerminalKill interrupts the worker+timer, marks completed, records a 'killed' completion, emits WorkerKilled(reason=terminal), and forks workspace cleanup (WorkspaceCleaned).
Coverage: test/orchestrator-loop.test.ts: reconcile terminal → kill worker, clean workspace, mark completed; test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: maps terminal → TerminalKill, active → UpdateActive, vanished/other → NeitherKill

Scenario: A worker emits no event for longer than stall_timeout_ms while still active in the tracker.
Expected Result: Stall detection takes precedence over tracker state: StallKill interrupts the worker, increments failureAttempts, and schedules a failure-backoff retry (or parks if the cap is crossed).
Coverage: test/orchestrator-loop.test.ts: reconcile stall → kill worker and schedule a failure retry; test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: kills a stalled worker (precedence over tracker state)

Scenario: stall_timeout_ms <= 0 (stall detection disabled).
Expected Result: No StallKill is ever emitted regardless of inactivity; the issue is classified by tracker state only (e.g. UpdateActive).
Coverage: test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: disables stall detection when timeout <= 0

Scenario: tracker.fetchIssueStatesByIds fails (refreshed === null) during a tick.
Expected Result: TrackerError is emitted and every running/retrying/abandoned issue is left untouched (no kills, no released retries); pending backoffs can still fire on their own timers.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: keeps all workers untouched when the refresh failed; leaves abandoned issues untouched when the tracker refresh failed

Scenario: Running issue is vanished (not returned) or in some other (non-active, non-terminal) state.
Expected Result: NeitherKill releases the worker without cleaning the workspace (release, not markCompleted); a still-active issue maps to UpdateActive (no-op beyond AgentEvent bookkeeping).
Coverage: test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: maps terminal → TerminalKill, active → UpdateActive, vanished/other → NeitherKill; property: never emits more actions than running workers

Scenario: A retry/continuation backoff issue (no worker, no stall clock) goes terminal mid-backoff.
Expected Result: classifyTrackerOnly reconciles it by tracker state: terminal -> TerminalKill+clean, vanished/other -> NeitherKill(release), active -> left alone so the pending retry fires; prevents one wasted dispatch (#17).
Coverage: test/orchestrator-loop.test.ts: a retrying issue that goes terminal mid-backoff is reconciled, not re-dispatched (Fixes #17)

Scenario: No budget block configured (absent ceiling) with arbitrary spend.
Expected Result: evaluateBudget returns configured=false, limit/remaining null, paused=false (inert); the snapshot omits the budget block entirely; dispatch behaves exactly as pre-#53.
Coverage: test/budget-pure.test.ts: an unchanged config (no budget block) decodes with an inert budget; no ceiling → inert: never paused, no limit/remaining; omits the budget block entirely when no ceiling is configured

Scenario: Spend exactly at ceiling, over ceiling, and just under (e.g. 499 vs 500 with max 500).
Expected Result: paused becomes true iff total_tokens >= ceiling (at and over); under the ceiling paused=false and remainingTokens counts down; remaining clamps to 0 and never goes negative.
Coverage: test/budget-pure.test.ts: a configured ceiling decodes and drives the guard; under ceiling → not paused, remaining counts down; exactly at ceiling → paused, remaining clamps to 0; over ceiling → paused, remaining never goes negative

Scenario: Spend under the ceiling end-to-end; a candidate dispatches normally.
Expected Result: New dispatch proceeds, no BudgetExceeded observation fires, the issue completes, and usage is folded into agent_totals.
Coverage: test/budget-gate.test.ts: under ceiling → dispatch proceeds, no pause observation

Scenario: Spend crosses the ceiling while a worker is in-flight and a new candidate appears.
Expected Result: NEW dispatch is withheld (the new candidate never runs) while the in-flight worker is never touched and completes+reconciles normally; only the dispatch gate is affected.
Coverage: test/budget-gate.test.ts: at/over ceiling → NEW dispatch withheld, but an in-flight worker is untouched and completes

Scenario: Budget stays exceeded across multiple ticks with a perpetual candidate present.
Expected Result: The BudgetExceeded(paused) observation fires exactly once on the transition into paused (latched on budgetPaused) and not again on subsequent paused ticks; each paused TickEnd reports dispatchSkipped=true.
Coverage: test/budget-gate.test.ts: paused observation fires once per transition, not every tick

Scenario: Budget ceiling raised (or budget cleared) via a ReloadConfig settings reload while paused.
Expected Result: Per source, ReloadConfig swaps liveConfig and mirrors the new ceiling into LiveBudget; the next tick re-evaluates against the new ceiling and can resume dispatch (transition back emits BudgetExceeded paused=false).
Coverage: Waived (subprocess/loop fault-injection path; covered via fake-script e2e + live smoke; deeper injection deferred)

Scenario: Budget pause is distinct from operator pause; controlState attribution.
Expected Result: Per source, dispatchPaused is operatorPaused||budgetPaused with pausedBy 'operator' taking priority over 'budget'; the two latches are independent and budget gating never touches operator state.
Coverage: Waived (subprocess/loop fault-injection path; covered via fake-script e2e + live smoke; deeper injection deferred)

Scenario: Encode a full OrchestratorState (running attempt with turn/failure_attempts/session_id, wall-clock retry, totals, vendor agent_rate_limits) to the versioned Schema JSON, write atomically via temp+rename, then read+decode back.
Expected Result: encode->write->read->decode is a fixed point: Dates round-trip as ISO and back to Date instances, additive continuity fields (turn=3, failure_attempts=1, session_id, retry kind=continuation) survive, and no state.json.tmp sibling remains after the rename.
Coverage: test/persistence.test.ts: encode → decode is a fixed point (Dates equal as Dates); test/persistence.test.ts: save → load round-trips through an atomic temp+rename write

Scenario: load runs against a directory with no state.json (cold start) vs a state.json containing invalid JSON (corruption).
Expected Result: Missing file -> Option.none (clean cold start, no throw). Corrupt file -> decode ParseError caught, original renamed aside to state.json.corrupt-<ts> (best-effort, preserved for diagnosis), load returns Option.none, never throws; original state.json no longer present.
Coverage: test/persistence.test.ts: missing checkpoint → Option.none (clean cold start); test/persistence.test.ts: corrupt checkpoint → rename-aside + Option.none, never throws (§2.4)

Scenario: save encounters a fault during the write path (encode/write/rename) — modelled via an unencodable agent_rate_limits (BigInt) that survives Schema.Unknown but faults whole-object JSON.stringify.
Expected Result: save is total and degrades ONLY agent_rate_limits to null (logged structured warning persistence_rate_limits_degraded); the rest of the checkpoint (running/retry/completed/totals + continuity) is still written. catchAll wraps the whole atomic write so a genuine IO fault is logged (persistence_save_failed), not raised — teardown can never crash.
Coverage: test/persistence.test.ts: an unencodable agent_rate_limits degrades to null; the rest of the checkpoint is still written; Gap: a real fs write/rename IO failure path (catchAll branch) is not directly exercised — only the encode-degrade path is

Scenario: A burst of mutations lands within one debounce window vs the window crossing; and a mutation followed by scope teardown without the debounce ever firing.
Expected Result: Debounce: before the window elapses no file exists; crossing the window fires exactly ONE write carrying the latest coalesced state (Queue.sliding(1) + single writer collapse 5 mutations to 1 scheduled flush, with no trailing window). On scope teardown the registered final-flush finalizer runs last (LIFO) and persists the latest state even if the debounce never fired.
Coverage: test/persistence.test.ts: debounce: a mutation is NOT written before the window; one write after; test/persistence.test.ts: debounce coalesces a burst of mutations into exactly one scheduled write; test/persistence.test.ts: guaranteed final flush on scope teardown even if debounce never fired

Scenario: save creates a fresh, not-yet-existing state dir and writes the checkpoint (which carries Copilot session_id capability handles) on a POSIX host.
Expected Result: State dir created mode 0700 and state.json mode 0600 (rename preserves the temp file's 0600 mode); group/other bits are 0 so session_ids are not world-readable. POSIX-only: assertions guarded off on win32 where modes are not enforced.
Coverage: test/persistence.test.ts: save creates the state dir 0700 and state.json 0600 (rename preserves mode); test/persistence.test.ts: session_ids are not world-readable: checkpoint mode excludes group/other

Scenario: decodePersisted is handed a malformed JSON string directly (not via a file), e.g. "{ not json".
Expected Result: Returns a Left/ParseError effect (no throw); the loader treats this as corruption -> clean start. Round-trips through the versioned KnownPersistedJson codec with the forward-only migrateToCurrent identity for version 1.
Coverage: test/persistence.test.ts: decode rejects a corrupt string with a ParseError (no throw)

Scenario: layerDurableOrchestratorStore is provided as a drop-in OrchestratorStore with no pre-existing checkpoint (cold start).
Expected Result: Store seeds from initialState(config): completed=[] and agent_totals=zeroTotals(); get/update/modify semantics are byte-identical to the plain store so loop.ts/snapshot need no edits.
Coverage: test/persistence.test.ts: layerDurableOrchestratorStore is a drop-in OrchestratorStore (cold start)

Scenario: A checkpoint with finite-but-degenerate or non-finite numeric totals (DEF-002 lineage): runtime_seconds produced non-finite upstream once corrupted the whole checkpoint on next boot.
Expected Result: Root-caused/fixed at the mapper boundary (ORC-F010 Copilot mapper) so totals are guaranteed finite before they reach the encoder; the persistence encoder still trusts finiteness and does NOT itself guard non-finite numbers — only agent_rate_limits is field-degraded. Residual risk per the inventory note.
Coverage: Waived (documented residual risk DEF-002; finiteness guaranteed at the ORC-F010 mapper boundary — see docs/qa/defect-log.md)

Scenario: Boot the real runOrchestrator on a checkpoint where i1 was running (1 clean turn done) on a still-active tracker issue; resume disabled (default).
Expected Result: Orphaned running -> due-immediately continuation retry; first tick reconciles but does NOT dispatch the orphan fresh (it is claimed); firing the due-now timer dispatches EXACTLY ONCE as turn 2 (turn_count+1), running FRESH (resumed=false, resumeSessionId=null), prompt contains 'Continue working on issue ORC-1 (turn 2)', then WorkerCompleted; state shows i1 completed, not running/retrying.
Coverage: test/restore-reconcile.test.ts: orphaned running → due-now continuation, dispatched EXACTLY ONCE

Scenario: Restored orphaned running issue that went TERMINAL (Done) or VANISHED (no longer returned) from the tracker while the daemon was down.
Expected Result: First-tick reconcile (FIFO-ordered before any restored RetryDue) kills it: terminal -> TerminalKill (WorkerKilled reason=terminal, marked completed, unclaimed); vanished -> NeitherKill (WorkerKilled reason=neither, released — not completed, not claimed). Never re-dispatched (agent runner invoked 0 times for i1).
Coverage: test/restore-reconcile.test.ts: orphaned running that went TERMINAL while down → killed, NOT re-dispatched; test/restore-reconcile.test.ts: orphaned running that VANISHED from the tracker → released, NOT re-dispatched

Scenario: Restore a pending retry whose seeded due_at_ms is a bogus large monotonic value (5_000_000) from the dead process — one already past-due (scheduled_at -5000 + delay 1000) and one in the future (scheduled_at 0 + delay 10_000).
Expected Result: Re-arm derives residual delay from WALL-CLOCK scheduled_at+delay_ms (Math.max(fireInstant-wallNow,0)), never the monotonic due_at_ms. Past-due fires on a 1ms advance (would need ~5_000_000ms if monotonic was used); future fires only after the 10_000ms wall offset (not at 9_999ms). Failure retry re-dispatches FRESH (turn 1).
Coverage: test/restore-reconcile.test.ts: pending retry already past-due → re-armed from WALL-CLOCK, fires immediately; test/restore-reconcile.test.ts: pending retry in the future → fires at the residual WALL-CLOCK offset (not monotonic)

Scenario: Restore a checkpoint with an abandoned (failure-cap-exhausted) issue that is still active on the tracker.
Expected Result: Abandoned issue is rebuilt as an inert registry record; NOT counted as orphan or re-armed retry (RestoredAfterRestart orphanedRunningConverted=0, reArmedRetries=0); stays parked/claimed (abandoned.attempts preserved), agent never invoked; when the tracker later flips terminal, the next tick reaps it exactly once (WorkerKilled reason=terminal, marked completed, unclaimed).
Coverage: test/restore-reconcile.test.ts: restored ACTIVE parked issue → stays claimed, NOT dispatched; reaped when terminal

Scenario: resume_sessions ON and a restored continuation carries a persisted session_id; vs resume_sessions OFF (default) with a session_id present on disk.
Expected Result: ON -> continuation dispatched WITH --resume (resumed=true, runner invoked with resumeSessionId='sess-abc', attempt=2). OFF -> session_id ignored, continuation runs FRESH (resumed=false, resumeSessionId=null) — byte-identical to the #41 baseline.
Coverage: test/restore-reconcile.test.ts: resume_sessions ON + restored session_id → continuation dispatched WITH --resume; test/restore-reconcile.test.ts: resume_sessions OFF (default) → restored continuation runs FRESH, session_id ignored

Scenario: resume_sessions ON but the restored session_id is stale and the agent rejects the resumed turn.
Expected Result: Resumed turn fails (WorkerFailed); self-heals via the normal failure-backoff retry which re-dispatches a FRESH continuation (resumed=false, resumeSessionId=null) against the on-disk workspace; issue still progresses to completion — never stranded, never crashed (2 runs: stale resume then fresh).
Coverage: test/restore-reconcile.test.ts: resume_sessions ON but resume REJECTED → self-heals to a FRESH continuation

Scenario: Cold start (no checkpoint) and corrupt checkpoint regression guards through the full loop.
Expected Result: Cold start emits NO RestoredAfterRestart, RestoreStatus.get stays null, snapshot omits the restore block, and a fresh candidate dispatches normally (turn 1). Corrupt checkpoint -> durable store loads none, boots clean (completed=[]), candidate dispatches normally, and the bad file is renamed to state.json.corrupt-<ts> (never re-poisons a later boot).
Coverage: test/restore-reconcile.test.ts: missing checkpoint → clean empty boot, normal dispatch (regression guard); test/restore-reconcile.test.ts: corrupt checkpoint → clean empty boot, never crashes (regression guard)

Scenario: Restore a checkpoint mixing one orphaned running, one re-armed retry, and 3 completed; verify the boot-time summary counts and durable RestoreStatus capture.
Expected Result: RestoredAfterRestart carries orphanedRunningConverted=1, reArmedRetries=1, restoredCompleted=3; completed bookkeeping restored immediately ([c1,c2,c3]); RestoreStatus captures the same one-shot summary durably (snapshot.restore = {at, orphaned_running_converted:1, rearmed_retries:1, restored_completed:3}).
Coverage: test/restore-reconcile.test.ts: RestoredAfterRestart carries the correct counts; test/restore-pure.test.ts: captures the boot-time summary on record

Scenario: Restore a pre-#42 / pre-#37 checkpoint where RunAttempt.session_id, RetryEntry.session_id, abandoned, or retry scheduled_at/delay_ms are ABSENT.
Expected Result: Versioned Schema decodes the older shape: absent session_id -> undefined (resume simply off), absent abandoned -> {}, and remainingWallMs falls back to 0 (due immediately, defensive) when scheduled_at/delay_ms are missing. Older files always decode; the daemon never writes an older version.
Coverage: test/domain.test.ts: RunAttempt decodes a pre-#42 checkpoint with session_id ABSENT; test/domain.test.ts: RetryEntry round-trips an optional session_id on a continuation retry (legacy branch); test/domain.test.ts: decodes a pre-upgrade checkpoint with `abandoned` ABSENT → defaults to {}

Scenario: formatObservation over the entire Observation union; ObserverLive emits structured logfmt records carrying the formatted annotations and level.
Expected Result: Every tag yields a non-empty message + a non-empty stable `event` annotation at level info|warn; issue context fields (issue_id, issue_identifier, session_id) present where the observation carries them; WorkerFailed logs at WARN. ObserverLive pipes the line through Effect logging with the annotations intact.
Coverage: test/live-observer.test.ts: covers every Observation tag with an event annotation; test/live-observer.test.ts: carries issue context fields where the observation has them; test/live-observer.test.ts: emits a log record carrying the formatted annotations

Scenario: AgentEvent humanization with a known tag (AgentMessage), an unknown/future tag, and a blank/whitespace/prototype-key tag.
Expected Result: Known tag -> friendly summary ('working') in the message while the raw event_tag annotation is retained; unknown tag -> raw label verbatim (fidelity over invention), never blank; blank/whitespace -> generic 'agent event'; prototype member names (toString, __proto__, ...) resolve via Object.hasOwn guard, not the prototype chain, so the total/never-blank contract holds.
Coverage: test/humanize.test.ts: falls back to the raw label for an unknown tag (never blank); test/humanize.test.ts: never returns blank, even for an empty or whitespace tag; test/humanize.test.ts: is total and never blank over an arbitrary string (property); test/live-observer.test.ts: humanizes the AgentEvent line while keeping the raw event_tag (#55)

Scenario: Very long (5000-char) and multi-line/secret-bearing observation messages and hook output flow into log lines, the recent-events ring, and truncate/truncateOneLine.
Expected Result: One-line fields are capped (formatObservation message <500, annotation <=121 at DEFAULT_MAX_LEN 120; ring message <=EVENT_MESSAGE_MAX 160); truncateOneLine collapses all whitespace/newlines/tabs to single spaces (no \n\r\t) before truncating so a record can never span lines or blow up; ellipsis counts toward the budget (output length never exceeds max).
Coverage: test/live-observer.test.ts: never overflows: long messages are truncated; test/recent-events.test.ts: truncates the message at ingestion and stamps a wall-clock ISO instant; test/glyphs.test.ts: property: truncate output never exceeds max; test/glyphs.test.ts: property: truncateOneLine output has no newline or tab characters

Scenario: Recent-events ring: monotonic seq + newest-last order, bounding at the cap (oldest dropped while seq climbs), and the deliberate drop of high-volume/cadence observations (AgentEvent, TickStart, TickEnd, Reconciled).
Expected Result: seq is 1-based monotonic, order newest-last; past the cap oldest envelopes are dropped while seq keeps climbing; toEventDraft returns null for the four cadence/chatter tags and a display-safe draft for every other tag; RestoredAfterRestart becomes one info 'restored' draft with the counts.
Coverage: test/recent-events.test.ts: assigns a monotonic 1-based seq and keeps newest-last order; test/recent-events.test.ts: bounds the ring to the cap, dropping oldest while seq keeps climbing; test/recent-events.test.ts: drops high-volume / cadence observations from the feed; test/recent-events.test.ts: restored-after-restart becomes one info 'restored' draft with the counts (#41)

Scenario: LiveActivity per-issue map: latest activity per issue with wall-clock instant, message omitted when absent, insertion-order eviction past the cap, and re-touch keeping an issue.
Expected Result: Latest event per issue wins with a stamped ISO `at`; absent message omits the key (exactOptionalPropertyTypes); the map is bounded (oldest insertion evicted) and re-touching an issue moves it to most-recent so a different issue is evicted instead. Stale entries for finished issues are harmless (only currently-running issues rendered).
Coverage: test/live-activity.test.ts: records the latest activity per issue with a wall-clock ISO instant; test/live-activity.test.ts: omits message when none is supplied; test/live-activity.test.ts: is bounded: oldest issues are evicted past the cap; test/live-activity.test.ts: re-touching an issue keeps it

Scenario: Glyph/status presentation: exhaustive phase->status rollup, the five canonical glyphs with ASCII fallbacks, and color decision under NO_COLOR / FORCE_COLOR / TTY / non-TTY.
Expected Result: PHASE_TO_STATUS maps every RunAttemptPhase to a valid status (total; new phase forces a compile error); each status exposes a non-empty glyph + ASCII fallback; shouldUseColor honors NO_COLOR (off) and FORCE_COLOR (on) over the TTY bit, defaults off for non-TTY/no-args; colorize wraps in SGR only when enabled and the label remains readable (color is never the only signal).
Coverage: test/glyphs.test.ts: maps every RunAttemptPhase to a valid status (exhaustive, total); test/glyphs.test.ts: emits ASCII fallbacks on request; test/glyphs.test.ts: honors NO_COLOR, FORCE_COLOR, and the TTY bit; test/glyphs.test.ts: colorized formatStatus still contains the readable label

Scenario: Snapshot enrichment is strictly additive: recent_events/recent_completed projected from rings, last_activity merged onto only the matching running issue, retry scheduled_at/delay_ms surfaced while due_at_ms is kept; and additive blocks (budget/restore/control) omitted when absent.
Expected Result: With no extras the new fields default empty and old fields stay byte-compatible (no last_activity on running, completed=[], counts intact); last_activity attaches only to the matching issue_id; retry carries scheduled_at(ISO)+delay_ms with due_at_ms unchanged, and omits them on a pre-#37 retry; budget/restore/control blocks are present ONLY when configured/after-real-restore/dispatch-withheld respectively (else absent).
Coverage: test/snapshot-enrichment.test.ts: is additive: with no extras the new fields are empty and old fields unchanged; test/snapshot-enrichment.test.ts: merges last_activity onto the matching running issue only; test/snapshot-enrichment.test.ts: retrying carries scheduled_at + delay_ms (ISO via JSON) while due_at_ms is kept; test/snapshot-enrichment.test.ts: a retry without the new fields omits them; test/restore-pure.test.ts: omits the restore block entirely on a cold start

Scenario: Agent summaries/messages must never echo agent payload text (prompts, tool args, issue content) into logs or snapshots.
Expected Result: humanizeAgentEvent maps by tag ONLY (display-only, never echoes payload); LiveActivity stores the raw tag plus the humanized one-line summary, not payload; recent-events drops per-token AgentEvent chatter from the feed entirely. PROJECT_BRIEF 9.2 constraint upheld by construction.
Coverage: test/recent-events.test.ts: preserves structured logging AND appends drafts to the shared ring (AgentEvent teed to LiveActivity with tag+humanized message, dropped from feed); Gap: no explicit negative test asserts that arbitrary agent payload text can never appear in a snapshot/log field

Scenario: GET /api/v1/state over a real loopback socket returns the snapshot for the current store plus additive rings/status blocks, token-free.
Expected Result: 200 with body byte-identical to JSON.stringify(toSnapshot(state, {recentEvents, recentCompleted, activity, budget=evaluateBudget(liveBudget, totals), operatorPaused, ...restore})); the read group has no bearer middleware (token-free) and the handler returns HttpServerResponse.json(toSnapshot(...)).orDie.
Coverage: test/cockpit-server.test.ts: is byte-identical to JSON.stringify(toSnapshot(...))

Scenario: A request to /api/v1/state (token-free read) and to a static path carries a non-loopback Host header (DNS-rebinding page re-resolving its name to 127.0.0.1).

Coverage: test/cockpit-server.test.ts: still serves a loopback Host (127.0.0.1:<port>) → 200

Scenario: Read the state endpoint when optional blocks are absent (unconfigured budget, no restore, dispatch not paused) vs configured budget / live-reloaded ceiling.
Expected Result: Snapshot stays strictly additive: budget block emitted only when budget.configured (projected from the LIVE LiveBudget ceiling, not the stale startup value); restore block only after a real restore; control block only when dispatch is withheld. Older readers tolerate omitted fields. The test fixture uses BudgetConfig.make({}) (unconfigured) so no budget block appears.
Coverage: test/cockpit-server.test.ts: is byte-identical to JSON.stringify(toSnapshot(...)) (unconfigured budget fixture); test/snapshot-enrichment.test.ts: is additive (omission proofs); Gap: no end-to-end test asserts the LIVE reloaded budget ceiling is reflected in /api/v1/state after a ReloadConfig

Scenario: A non-API route is requested when no SPA build exists under the static dir (Phase-1 reality / tracker observability blocks present but UI absent).
Expected Result: Graceful 404 whose body references /api/v1 (static handler index fallback degrades cleanly); the API still owns its own 404s for /api/* paths. A bind failure (port in use) logs cockpit_server_error and idles without taking down orchestration.
Coverage: test/cockpit-server.test.ts: serves a graceful 404 for a non-API route when the SPA is not built; Gap: bind-failure-does-not-crash-orchestration path is not unit-tested (Live/manual only)

Scenario: Mutating control endpoints (used to contrast the token-free read) are hit with no token and with a good token from a cross-origin page; and security headers on read/static responses.
Expected Result: Control endpoints (not the read) require bearer auth: no token -> 401; good token + cross-origin Origin -> 403. The read endpoint deliberately carries NO bearer middleware. Anti-framing/nosniff headers (x-frame-options DENY, CSP frame-ancestors 'none', x-content-type-options nosniff) are added to BOTH API and static responses.
Coverage: test/cockpit-server.test.ts: rejects a mutating request with no token → 401; test/cockpit-server.test.ts: rejects a good token from a cross-origin page → 403; test/cockpit-server.test.ts: adds anti-framing and nosniff headers to API and static responses

Scenario: The state read assembles the snapshot from the store plus five observability services (events, completions, activity, restore, control, live-budget) on every poll.
Expected Result: Each underlying read is a cheap concurrent Ref.get / bounded ring list (rings capped: events 200, completions 50, activity 256) so the projection is bounded regardless of daemon uptime; toSnapshot is a pure projection. No per-request unbounded work.
Coverage: Live/manual: no explicit performance/latency assertion exists; bounding is covered indirectly by the ring-cap tests (recent-events/live-activity), not by a /api/v1/state benchmark

Scenario: An issue fails past max_failure_retries=1 (failureAttempts crosses the budget).
Expected Result: Instead of another backoff the loop abandons it: workspace cleanup is awaited (WorkspaceCleaned precedes WorkerAbandoned), an AbandonedIssue (attempts=2) is recorded in state.abandoned, the claim is held, running/retry cleared, and WorkerAbandoned is emitted.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-loop.test.ts: failure retry cap parks the issue and prevents re-dispatch burn

Scenario: max_failure_retries=0 (park on the very first failure, fail-fast).
Expected Result: The first (and only) failure crosses max=0 immediately: abandoned.attempts=1, no retry is ever scheduled, the issue stays claimed, and a queued second script never runs.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-loop.test.ts: max_failure_retries=0 parks on the FIRST failure (fail-fast, no retry)

Scenario: A parked issue stays active in the tracker across later polls.
Expected Result: It stays claimed so selection never re-picks it; reconcile classifies it tracker-only and leaves an active parked issue alone (no dispatch, no UpdateActive); the runner is never invoked again.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-loop.test.ts: failure retry cap parks the issue and prevents re-dispatch burn; test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: parked (abandoned) issues reconcile by tracker state only — no stall, no UpdateActive

Scenario: A parked issue is later moved terminal (or vanishes) in the tracker.
Expected Result: Terminal -> TerminalKill: reaped, marked completed, claim cleared, abandoned entry removed; vanished/other-state -> NeitherKill (released without cleanup). Reaping uses tracker-state classification, never re-dispatch.
Coverage: test/restore-reconcile.test.ts: restored ACTIVE parked issue → stays claimed, NOT dispatched; reaped when terminal; test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: parked (abandoned) issues reconcile by tracker state only

Scenario: Restart with abandoned entries present in the checkpoint.
Expected Result: abandoned defaults to {} so pre-existing checkpoints decode; restore rebuilds an inert registry record (failureAttempts from attempts), does NOT count it as orphan/re-armed retry, and the first tick reconciles it by tracker state.
Coverage: test/restore-reconcile.test.ts: restored ACTIVE parked issue → stays claimed, NOT dispatched; reaped when terminal

Scenario: State-transition invariant: running/retry_attempts/abandoned are mutually exclusive and every abandoned key is also claimed.
Expected Result: abandon() omits the id from running and retry_attempts, adds it to abandoned, and keeps the claim; setRunning/setRetry conversely omit it from abandoned; markCompleted/release clear all three plus the claim.
Coverage: test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts: abandon parks the issue (clears running/retry, keeps claim)

Scenario: Workspace cleanup fails (best-effort) at abandon time, or the workspace is already null.
Expected Result: Per source, cleanupWorkspaceEffect catches the failure and logs a warning, then the issue is still parked; when rec.workspace is already null no cleanup is attempted. Cleanup is awaited before persisting so a forked rm can't leak the dir.
Coverage: Waived (subprocess/loop fault-injection path; covered via fake-script e2e + live smoke; deeper injection deferred)

Scenario: AbandonedIssue.attempts schema bound (PositiveInt, always >= 1 = max+1).

Coverage: test/domain.test.ts: rejects a non-positive attempts (already covered)
